zotero.abstractNote: Causal representation learning aims to recover the underlying causal variables and their causal structure from high-dimensional observations. Existing methods typically assume that all causal variables are observed in the data. However, in many real-world scenarios, some causal variables are unobserved, leading to partial observability. In this paper, we study the identifiability of causal representations under partial observability using multi-view data. We show that, under certain conditions, the latent causal variables can be identified up to a component-wise transformation, even when some variables are not observed in any view. We propose a novel framework, Multi-View Causal Representation Learning (MVCRL), to learn these representations and demonstrate its effectiveness on synthetic and real-world datasets.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations
